name: KafkaJS
what_it_does: Modern Apache Kafka client for Node.js. KafkaJS provides a clean async API for producing and consuming Kafka messages in Node.js/TypeScript applications. Supports all Kafka features: consumer groups, transactions, exactly-once semantics, admin operations, and schema registry integration. The standard Kafka client for agent systems built on Node.js.
use_cases: Build event-driven agent architectures with Kafka as the message backbone for agent task distribution
Consume Kafka events in Node.js agent consumers with consumer group coordination and offset management
Produce agent output events to Kafka topics for downstream processing in agent event pipelines
Implement exactly-once agent workflows using Kafka transactions for financial and critical data processing
Connect agent services to Kafka-based data platforms (Confluent, MSK, Redpanda) for real-time data processing
not_for: RabbitMQ or Redis queues — use aio-pika or ioredis for those brokers
Simple job queuing without Kafka infrastructure — use BullMQ or arq for simpler use cases
Python or Go Kafka clients — use confluent-kafka-python or sarama for those languages
best_when: You're building Node.js agent services that consume from or produce to Apache Kafka (or compatible: Confluent, MSK, Redpanda).
avoid_when: You don't have Kafka infrastructure — Kafka's operational complexity is significant; use simpler queue solutions when possible.

auth.notes: Kafka authentication via SASL (PLAIN, SCRAM, OAUTHBEARER) or SSL/mTLS configured in Kafka client constructor.

pricing.notes: KafkaJS is free. Kafka infrastructure (Confluent Cloud, MSK, Redpanda) has its own pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Limited by Kafka broker capacity and partition count

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: KafkaJSError hierarchy with specific error types (KafkaJSConnectionError, KafkaJSOffsetOutOfRange, etc.). Consumer crash errors include topic/partition/offset context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Kafka transactions enable exactly-once semantics. Consumer groups track offsets for at-least-once delivery. Manual offset management for idempotent processing.
known_agent_gotchas: Consumer group rebalances pause consumption — agents must handle rebalance pause gracefully and not process messages during coordinator transitions
eachMessage vs eachBatch: eachMessage processes one message at a time, eachBatch provides batch for parallel processing; choose based on throughput requirements
Kafka offsets are committed manually or auto — with autoCommit=true, messages are committed before processing completes; use manual commit for exactly-once semantics
KafkaJS 2.x requires Node.js 14+ — older versions may fail with cryptic errors
Transactional producers require transaction.id configured — transactions add latency; only use for exactly-once guarantees when required
Schema Registry integration (Confluent Avro/Protobuf) requires additional @kafkajs/confluent-schema-registry package — not built into KafkaJS core

security_score_components.security_notes: SASL/SSL supported for Kafka authentication. Credentials must use environment variables. TLS enforced for Confluent Cloud and AWS MSK connections.
